name: Friday Night Pot Roast (Meat)
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Top or bottom round, 2-inch thick
1 each bell pepper, seeded and chopped
6 sprigs parsley and dill, plus 1 bay leaf
1 1/2 tbsp plain or seasoned olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Place meat in a large, narrow pot or bowl that will just hold it, leaving room for liquid (cut the meat in half if necessary).
2. In a medium saucepan, combine onion, carrots, bell pepper, garlic, shallots, spice bag, cloves, spices, apple juice, and water. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat to simmering and cook for 3 minutes. Stir in wine.
4. Pour over meat and cover. Refrigerate overnight.
5. Preheat oven to 300°F. Drain the meat, pushing off vegetables into another bowl. Remove and reserve spice bag. Strain marinade into a bowl, reserving solids.
6. Heat oil in a heavy Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown meat all over; add drained vegetables. Stir and cook with meat until they begin to soften.
7. Add reserved marinade, spice bag, and tomato puree, turning the meat and spooning several times with sauce. Cover and cook on moderate heat for 5 minutes.
8. Position oven rack in the lower third of the oven. Bake for 1 hour. Turn meat and baste; repeat turning and basting three more times until meat is tender (3½ to 4 hours).
9. Remove pot from oven and let stand 30 minutes. Discard spice bag.
10. Transfer meat to a bowl with cover. Place pot over medium-high heat and bring sauce to a boil. Cook, uncovered, until reduced by one-quarter. Let cool; then pour into another container with a tight lid. Refrigerate meat and sauce until well chilled.
11. Cut away any congealed fat from the sauce. Pour sauce into a wide heavy-bottom saucepan. Heat until bubbling. Sprinkle with salt.
12. Cut meat against grain into 1/4-inch slices. Immerse in sauce and heat through. Arrange meat on warmed serving platter in a neat pattern. Spoon some gravy over; serve remainder in gravy boat. Garnish platter with watercress.
